subject: english
topic_title: Vocabulary Development: Words Associated with Agriculture
learning_objectives: Define common terms used in crop farming and general agriculture
Define terms used in livestock farming

sections.heading: General Agricultural Terms
sections.content:
agrarian economy – a nation whose economy is predominantly based on agriculture
fallow – to leave land uncultivated for a period to restore fertility
orchard – a piece of land on which fruit trees are grown
seedling – a young plant grown from seed
barn – a building for storing farm produce, especially yams
cash crop – a crop grown for sale rather than for consumption
arable land – land that is good for tilling or ploughing
mechanised farm – agriculture practised with the use of machines
nursery – a place where young trees and plants are raised for transplanting or sale
manure – fertiliser made from dung
bumper harvest – an unusually large quantity of crop gathered at the end of a season
tendril – the soft, thin part of a climbing plant that attaches to a support
crop rotation – the practice of changing the crops planted on the same land each season
agricultural loan – a loan from government or bank to support farming projects
hybrid fruits – fruits produced from two plants of different species
amortise – to repay borrowed money in instalments

sections.heading: Livestock Terms
sections.content:
livestock – domestic animals raised for food or labour
ranches – farms or confined spaces for rearing animals
beef – meat from cattle or a cow
animal droppings – dung or faeces of animals or birds
broiler – young chickens reared for eating
dairy products – food items made from milk, e.g. cheese and butter
pasture – grassland on which grass-eating animals feed
pullet – a young hen
vaccinate – to inject an animal with a vaccine as protection against illness
veterinarian / veterinary doctor – a person who treats animals

sections.heading: Fishery Terms
sections.content:
fish-net – a net used to catch fish
cane pole and line – tools used to catch fish
ripples – small waves on the water's surface after a net or pole is thrown in
bait – anything used to attract fish so it may be caught (e.g. earthworm)
float – a piece of wood or cork at the end of a fishing-line that moves when a fish bites
trawler – a boat used for fishing that drags a fish-net behind it
fish pond – a small artificial area of still water where fish are nurtured for consumption
fishery – a part of a sea or river where fish are caught in large quantities; the industry of catching fish
fish farm – a place where fish are bred commercially
